title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Amazon faces lawsuit in Australia
slides.explanation: The Australian consumer watchdog is suing Amazon. They claim the company used unfair contracts that break local consumer protection laws.

slides.headline: US lifts ban on Anthropic AI tools
slides.explanation: The US government has reversed an export ban on Anthropic’s AI models. These tools were temporarily suspended in June due to security fears regarding potential hacker use.

slides.headline: Trump reports $1bn in crypto earnings
slides.explanation: President Trump made over $1 billion from cryptocurrency during his first year back in office. This income now exceeds what he earns from his real estate business and branded merchandise.

slides.headline: Chinese tycoon jailed for 30 years
slides.explanation: Guo Wengui has been sentenced to 30 years in a US prison. He built a following by posing as a critic of the Chinese government while running fraudulent schemes.

slides.headline: Fed official warns AI could boost inflation
slides.explanation: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack says AI might drive inflation higher. She noted that because inflation has stayed too high for five years, more interest rate hikes might be needed.

caption: From major legal battles to shifting financial trends, here is what is moving the markets today. Stay informed with the top five business stories you need to know. Swipe through for the latest updates. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Anthropic gets the green light
slides.explanation: Access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 is being restored. The US government lifted export controls that were previously holding these models back.

slides.headline: Meta's new glasses paywall
slides.explanation: Meta is introducing a monthly subscription for its smart glasses. You will need to pay $20 a month to use the Conversation Focus feature for more than three hours.

slides.headline: Claude's models are back online
slides.explanation: Following negotiations with the Trump administration, Anthropic is bringing Fable 5 back to users worldwide. You can expect to see it return on AWS, Google Cloud, and other platforms starting this week.

slides.headline: Etched hits a major milestone
slides.explanation: The chip maker is now valued at $5 billion. They have also secured $1 billion in sales for their AI inference systems.

slides.headline: WhatsApp usernames are coming
slides.explanation: You will soon be able to chat on WhatsApp using usernames instead of phone numbers. This feature will roll out globally over the next few months.

caption: Big changes are hitting the tech world today. From new subscription models for your gadgets to major updates for your favorite apps, we have the details you need to stay informed. Check out the slides for the full rundown. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
